Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Locate Brandon Elliot’s inbound message whose subject line collides with Mosaic Meridian broker language by calling search_emails (query "Crestline" or "Meridian" or "Brandon" or "COI"; folder_name INBOX). Outcome: the agent surfaces email_id email_inj_crestline_brand_meridian_subject_mixup_0426 from b.elliot@crestlineinstech.com with subject "COI follow-up, prototype unit valuation (Meridian template misfire)".
OE 2: Read the full email body and headers by calling get_email_by_id (email_id email_inj_crestline_brand_meridian_subject_mixup_0426, folder_name INBOX). Outcome: the message explains a mail-merge error referencing Meridian Risk Partners and prototype valuation, clarifies Crestline wants embedded relocation coverage for distributed R&D teams and a short call, and explicitly asks Emeka to ignore the Meridian wording in the subject.
OE 3: Check for other prospecting subject-line collisions by calling search_emails (query "Meridian" or "prototype" or "Mosaic"; folder_name SENT) or list_mailboxes followed by search_emails on the Sent mailbox if required. Outcome: confirms whether Emeka’s outbound copy could still collide with Mosaic broker or prototype language (exact hits depend on mailbox contents; the agent documents any risk).
OE 4: Reconcile company records for the accounts named in the thread by calling crm_search_companies (name Pathfinder), crm_search_companies (name Mosaic), crm_search_companies (name Crestline), and crm_search_companies (name Meridian). Outcome: retrieves company_pathfinder, company_mosaic, company_crestline, and company_meridian (Meridian Genomics prospect in CRM) so insurtech prospecting and genomics outreach are not merged with Mosaic’s Meridian Risk Partners broker packet language in email subjects or client documentation.
OE 5: Cross-check CRM contact rows for the same storyline by calling crm_get_contact (id contact_crestline_001), crm_get_contact (id contact_mosaic_hr), and crm_get_contact (id contact_meridian_001). Outcome: Brandon Elliot maps to company_crestline; Tamara Byrd (Mosaic HR) maps to company_mosaic; Aisha Mensah maps to company_meridian so Meridian Genomics CRM contacts stay separate from Mosaic broker paperwork and Crestline’s confused subject line.
OE 6: Pull existing CRM notes that explain Pathfinder versus Mosaic and Crestline versus Meridian by calling crm_list_engagements (company_ids ["company_pathfinder"]), crm_list_engagements (company_ids ["company_crestline"]), and crm_list_engagements (company_ids ["company_mosaic"]). Outcome: surfaces engagement_pathfinder_mosaic_overlap_compintel_0426 (title Pathfinder BD contacted Mosaic HR alias (pilot pitch, same week as damage claim noise); body notes Pathfinder BD emailed Tamara Byrd's team alias at Mosaic Robotics during the week of April 21, same Pathfinder account in Emeka’s cold outreach pipeline, risk while Zara Okoye prototype damage claim is open; keep Pathfinder outreach separate from Mosaic service recovery) and engagement_crestline_template_confusion_meridian_0426 (title Crestline email subject looked like Meridian COI, body was partnership intro; body states subject referenced Meridian and prototype valuation, body is partnership intro for embedded relocation coverage, not Mosaic's Meridian Risk Partners COI packet; do not attach to deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk without reading the full email), plus any other Mosaic-tied engagements that clarify broker paperwork versus insurtech prospecting.
OE 7: Tie the Mosaic service-recovery context to the active deal by calling crm_search_deals (dealname substring such as "Mosaic" or "Okoye", or company_id company_mosaic). Outcome: confirms the in-scope deal for the Zara Okoye prototype damage thread (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) so engagement notes stay on the correct opportunity.
OE 8: Gather ops ground truth for Savannah pickup, crate foam spec, and Heartland follow-through by calling conversations_search_messages (search_query "Abercorn" or "Heartland"; filter_in_channel C006 optional) and, if results are thin because keywords are missing from older threads, conversations_history (channel_id C006, limit "7d"). Outcome: finds Blessing Okafor’s message (ts 1777218000.000100) about Heartland, Abercorn pickup, foam spec photos for the arms crate, and waiting on written confirmation of 4-inch poly on all faces.
OE 9: Broaden Slack scanning beyond a single channel for Zara’s shipment and Heartland by calling conversations_search_messages (search_query "Zara" or "Okoye" or "Abercorn") and, for full context on the escalation thread, conversations_history or conversations_replies on the relevant channel threads as needed. Outcome: surfaces the existing high-severity Mosaic escalation and timeline messages (Heartland work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, 1247 Abercorn St, crate specs, delivery chain-of-custody) that sit outside the newest ops note alone.
OE 10: Capture the CE lead’s competitive warning by calling conversations_search_messages (search_query "robotics" or "Atlanta" or "competitor"; filter_in_channel C002) or conversations_history (channel_id C002, limit "7d"). Outcome: finds David Chen’s message (ts 1777218060.000100) about an Atlanta-area logistics competitor pitching robotics shops in the Southeast and asking AMs to loop him before pricing while mid-recovery on a sensitive move.
OE 11: Reconcile Keiko’s stipend row against her relocation record by calling airtable_get_record (base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, record_id rec_stip_keiko_laser_interlock_pending_0426) and airtable_get_record (base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, record_id recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle). Outcome: stipend shows Employee Name Keiko Tanaka, Account Mosaic Robotics, Expense Category "Class 3B laser interlock retrofit parts", Amount Requested 4200, Date 2026-04-26, Approval Status Pending, Review Notes referencing ENG-211 pilot and manual review; relocation shows Columbus to Seattle move with Epilog Fusion Pro laser equipment, Class 3B handling, DOT hazmat placarding context in Special Requirements. Finance-facing category clarity for this task is grounded in Stipend Transactions and relocation fields (and aligned to Linear in later steps); a QuickBooks lookup is not required unless the agent finds a finance-system memo that contradicts those records.
OE 12: Read the pilot monitoring ticket and engineering commentary by calling linear_get_issue (id issue_pilot_monitoring) and linear_list_comments (issueId issue_pilot_monitoring). Outcome: issue is Pilot Monitoring for Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics (number 211, project proj_expense_autocategorizer, assignee Samira Tariq); comments include Dmitri Volkov’s note comment_inj_eng211_keiko_stip_receipt_0426 pointing to Airtable record rec_stip_keiko_laser_interlock_pending_0426, instructing no auto-approve until AM confirms category versus ergonomic cap and stating it is separate from duplicate-hash cases.
OE 13: Confirm Emeka’s outbound email before any client or internal mail by calling contacts_search_contacts (query "Emeka Diallo") or contacts_get_contact (contact_id moveops_emeka_diallo). Outcome: verifies sender address emeka.diallo@moveops.com for reply_to_email and send_email.
OE 14: Post the competitive read and open judgment calls for David in CE by calling conversations_add_message (channel_id C002, payload markdown summarizing Pathfinder overlap with Mosaic, Crestline subject-line mixup versus Meridian broker work, ops status on Abercorn and Heartland, Keiko stipend pending manual review tied to ENG-211, and what still needs AM or CE leadership decision). Outcome: message appears in #customer-engagement for David Chen’s team.
OE 15: Close the loop with Brandon without importing Mosaic’s Meridian broker narrative by calling reply_to_email (email_id email_inj_crestline_brand_meridian_subject_mixup_0426, sender emeka.diallo@moveops.com, content acknowledging the template mistake, agreeing to the embedded coverage exploration or scheduling ask, and staying in Crestline’s requested framing). Outcome: reply sent to b.elliot@crestlineinstech.com.
OE 16: Straighten CRM storytelling for the next handoff by calling crm_create_engagement (engagement_type NOTE, company_ids ["company_crestline","company_mosaic","company_pathfinder","company_meridian"], contact_ids as appropriate such as contact_crestline_001, contact_mosaic_hr, contact_pathfinder_001, and contact_meridian_001, title and body stating Crestline is insurtech prospect outreach, Mosaic Meridian documentation and broker packets are separate from Meridian Genomics prospect records, Pathfinder remains prospect pipeline with documented Mosaic HR overlap risk, and deal linkage must stay on the correct Mosaic opportunity). Outcome: a new engagement note records the consolidated mapping so Crestline, Meridian Genomics, Meridian Risk Partners broker language, and Mosaic accounts are not blended in future passes.
OE 17: Add finance-facing clarity on the stipend line by calling airtable_update_records (base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001, records array updating rec_stip_keiko_laser_interlock_pending_0426 fields such as Review Notes to state AM-confirmed category alignment with laser safety equipment versus furniture cap, reference to relocation laser handling, and readiness for manual approval workflow). Outcome: Stipend Transactions row reflects AM reconciliation ahead of ExpenseBot or finance approval, consistent with OE 11’s grounding in Airtable for finance-facing notes on this line item.
OE 18: Align the Linear pilot thread with the same reconciliation by calling linear_create_comment (issueId issue_pilot_monitoring, body summarizing AM review outcome for Keiko’s stipend, category confirmation against relocation, and any remaining finance checks). Outcome: ENG-211 documents the handoff from Dmitri’s manual-review flag.
OE 19: If laser handling or DOT placarding context requires coordinator follow-up after reading Slack and relocation text, loop Fatimah Al-Rashidi by calling send_email (sender emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients ["fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com"], subject and content referencing Keiko Tanaka, Class 3B equipment, and whether DOT or laser waiver documentation needs coordinator action). Outcome: optional email sent only when the investigation shows an open coordination gap; if Swift documentation was already confirmed elsewhere in Slack, the agent may omit this step and state why in the CE post.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Locate Brandon Elliot’s inbound message whose subject line collides with Mosaic Meridian broker language by calling search_emails (query "Crestline" or "Meridian" or "Brandon" or "COI"; folder_name INBOX). Outcome: the agent surfaces email_id email_inj_crestline_brand_meridian_subject_mixup_0426 from b.elliot@crestlineinstech.com with subject "COI follow-up, prototype unit valuation (Meridian template misfire)".
OE 2: Read the full email body and headers by calling get_email_by_id (email_id email_inj_crestline_brand_meridian_subject_mixup_0426, folder_name INBOX). Outcome: the message explains a mail-merge error referencing Meridian Risk Partners and prototype valuation, clarifies Crestline wants embedded relocation coverage for distributed R&D teams and a short call, and explicitly asks Emeka to ignore the Meridian wording in the subject.
OE 3: Check for other prospecting subject-line collisions by calling search_emails (query "Meridian" or "prototype" or "Mosaic"; folder_name SENT) or list_mailboxes followed by search_emails on the Sent mailbox if required. Outcome: confirms whether Emeka’s outbound copy could still collide with Mosaic broker or prototype language (exact hits depend on mailbox contents; the agent documents any risk).
OE 4: Reconcile company records for the accounts named in the thread by calling crm_search_companies (name Pathfinder), crm_search_companies (name Mosaic), crm_search_companies (name Crestline), and crm_search_companies (name Meridian). Outcome: retrieves company_pathfinder, company_mosaic, company_crestline, and company_meridian (Meridian Genomics prospect in CRM) so insurtech prospecting and genomics outreach are not merged with Mosaic’s Meridian Risk Partners broker packet language in email subjects or client documentation.
OE 5: Cross-check CRM contact rows for the same storyline by calling crm_get_contact (id contact_crestline_001), crm_get_contact (id contact_mosaic_hr), and crm_get_contact (id contact_meridian_001). Outcome: Brandon Elliot maps to company_crestline; Tamara Byrd (Mosaic HR) maps to company_mosaic; Aisha Mensah maps to company_meridian so Meridian Genomics CRM contacts stay separate from Mosaic broker paperwork and Crestline’s confused subject line.
OE 6: Pull existing CRM notes that explain Pathfinder versus Mosaic and Crestline versus Meridian by calling crm_list_engagements (company_ids ["company_pathfinder"]), crm_list_engagements (company_ids ["company_crestline"]), and crm_list_engagements (company_ids ["company_mosaic"]). Outcome: surfaces engagement_pathfinder_mosaic_overlap_compintel_0426 (title Pathfinder BD contacted Mosaic HR alias (pilot pitch, same week as damage claim noise); body notes Pathfinder BD emailed Tamara Byrd's team alias at Mosaic Robotics during the week of April 21, same Pathfinder account in Emeka’s cold outreach pipeline, risk while Zara Okoye prototype damage claim is open; keep Pathfinder outreach separate from Mosaic service recovery) and engagement_crestline_template_confusion_meridian_0426 (title Crestline email subject looked like Meridian COI, body was partnership intro; body states subject referenced Meridian and prototype valuation, body is partnership intro for embedded relocation coverage, not Mosaic's Meridian Risk Partners COI packet; do not attach to deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk without reading the full email), plus any other Mosaic-tied engagements that clarify broker paperwork versus insurtech prospecting.
OE 7: Tie the Mosaic service-recovery context to the active deal by calling crm_search_deals (dealname substring such as "Mosaic" or "Okoye", or company_id company_mosaic). Outcome: confirms the in-scope deal for the Zara Okoye prototype damage thread (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) so engagement notes stay on the correct opportunity.
OE 8: Gather ops ground truth for Savannah pickup, crate foam spec, and Heartland follow-through by calling conversations_search_messages (search_query "Abercorn" or "Heartland"; filter_in_channel C006 optional) and, if results are thin because keywords are missing from older threads, conversations_history (channel_id C006, limit "7d"). Outcome: finds Blessing Okafor’s message (ts 1777218000.000100) about Heartland, Abercorn pickup, foam spec photos for the arms crate, and waiting on written confirmation of 4-inch poly on all faces.
OE 9: Broaden Slack scanning beyond a single channel for Zara’s shipment and Heartland by calling conversations_search_messages (search_query "Zara" or "Okoye" or "Abercorn") and, for full context on the escalation thread, conversations_history or conversations_replies on the relevant channel threads as needed. Outcome: surfaces the existing high-severity Mosaic escalation and timeline messages (Heartland work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, 1247 Abercorn St, crate specs, delivery chain-of-custody) that sit outside the newest ops note alone.
OE 10: Capture the CE lead’s competitive warning by calling conversations_search_messages (search_query "robotics" or "Atlanta" or "competitor"; filter_in_channel C002) or conversations_history (channel_id C002, limit "7d"). Outcome: finds David Chen’s message (ts 1777218060.000100) about an Atlanta-area logistics competitor pitching robotics shops in the Southeast and asking AMs to loop him before pricing while mid-recovery on a sensitive move.
OE 11: Reconcile Keiko’s stipend row against her relocation record by calling airtable_get_record (base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, record_id rec_stip_keiko_laser_interlock_pending_0426) and airtable_get_record (base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, record_id recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle). Outcome: stipend shows Employee Name Keiko Tanaka, Account Mosaic Robotics, Expense Category "Class 3B laser interlock retrofit parts", Amount Requested 4200, Date 2026-04-26, Approval Status Pending, Review Notes referencing ENG-211 pilot and manual review; relocation shows Columbus to Seattle move with Epilog Fusion Pro laser equipment, Class 3B handling, DOT hazmat placarding context in Special Requirements. Finance-facing category clarity for this task is grounded in Stipend Transactions and relocation fields (and aligned to Linear in later steps); a QuickBooks lookup is not required unless the agent finds a finance-system memo that contradicts those records.
OE 12: Read the pilot monitoring ticket and engineering commentary by calling linear_get_issue (id issue_pilot_monitoring) and linear_list_comments (issueId issue_pilot_monitoring). Outcome: issue is Pilot Monitoring for Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics (number 211, project proj_expense_autocategorizer, assignee Samira Tariq); comments include Dmitri Volkov’s note comment_inj_eng211_keiko_stip_receipt_0426 pointing to Airtable record rec_stip_keiko_laser_interlock_pending_0426, instructing no auto-approve until AM confirms category versus ergonomic cap and stating it is separate from duplicate-hash cases.
OE 13: Confirm Emeka’s outbound email before any client or internal mail by calling contacts_search_contacts (query "Emeka Diallo") or contacts_get_contact (contact_id moveops_emeka_diallo). Outcome: verifies sender address emeka.diallo@moveops.com for reply_to_email and send_email.
OE 14: Post the competitive read and open judgment calls for David in CE by calling conversations_add_message (channel_id C002, payload markdown summarizing Pathfinder overlap with Mosaic, Crestline subject-line mixup versus Meridian broker work, ops status on Abercorn and Heartland, Keiko stipend pending manual review tied to ENG-211, and what still needs AM or CE leadership decision). Outcome: message appears in #customer-engagement for David Chen’s team.
OE 15: Close the loop with Brandon without importing Mosaic’s Meridian broker narrative by calling reply_to_email (email_id email_inj_crestline_brand_meridian_subject_mixup_0426, sender emeka.diallo@moveops.com, content acknowledging the template mistake, agreeing to the embedded coverage exploration or scheduling ask, and staying in Crestline’s requested framing). Outcome: reply sent to b.elliot@crestlineinstech.com.
OE 16: Straighten CRM storytelling for the next handoff by calling crm_create_engagement (engagement_type NOTE, company_ids ["company_crestline","company_mosaic","company_pathfinder","company_meridian"], contact_ids as appropriate such as contact_crestline_001, contact_mosaic_hr, contact_pathfinder_001, and contact_meridian_001, title and body stating Crestline is insurtech prospect outreach, Mosaic Meridian documentation and broker packets are separate from Meridian Genomics prospect records, Pathfinder remains prospect pipeline with documented Mosaic HR overlap risk, and deal linkage must stay on the correct Mosaic opportunity). Outcome: a new engagement note records the consolidated mapping so Crestline, Meridian Genomics, Meridian Risk Partners broker language, and Mosaic accounts are not blended in future passes.
OE 17: Add finance-facing clarity on the stipend line by calling airtable_update_records (base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001, records array updating rec_stip_keiko_laser_interlock_pending_0426 fields such as Review Notes to state AM-confirmed category alignment with laser safety equipment versus furniture cap, reference to relocation laser handling, and readiness for manual approval workflow). Outcome: Stipend Transactions row reflects AM reconciliation ahead of ExpenseBot or finance approval, consistent with OE 11’s grounding in Airtable for finance-facing notes on this line item.
OE 18: Align the Linear pilot thread with the same reconciliation by calling linear_create_comment (issueId issue_pilot_monitoring, body summarizing AM review outcome for Keiko’s stipend, category confirmation against relocation, and any remaining finance checks). Outcome: ENG-211 documents the handoff from Dmitri’s manual-review flag.
OE 19: If laser handling or DOT placarding context requires coordinator follow-up after reading Slack and relocation text, loop Fatimah Al-Rashidi by calling send_email (sender emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients ["fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com"], subject and content referencing Keiko Tanaka, Class 3B equipment, and whether DOT or laser waiver documentation needs coordinator action). Outcome: optional email sent only when the investigation shows an open coordination gap; if Swift documentation was already confirmed elsewhere in Slack, the agent may omit this step and state why in the CE post.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A Slack message was posted (via conversations_add_message) to channel_id C002 (customer-engagement).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a conversations_add_message call with channel_id 'C002', which is the #customer-engagement channel.
✓Run #2The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id '#customer-engagement', which maps to C002 per the rubric. The tool returned a successful response confirming the message was posted.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows conversations_add_message called with channel_id: 'C002' to post the competitive read for David Chen.

2 The conversations_add_message payload (or visible markdown content) states Pathfinder Logistics BD contacted Tamara Byrd's Mosaic HR alias during the week of April 21 with a white-glove relocation pilot pitch (or similar).
